blue_oceans.notes: 4.83M entrepreneurship community. YC Startup School and MasterClass offer passive consumption; no app teaches the science of startup mechanics as a daily habit. Gap: one startup science concept per day (what the Lean Startup Build-Measure-Learn loop is and how Dropbox used a fake MVP to validate before building, how product-market fit is measured using Sean Ellis's 40% rule vs. NPS vs. cohort retention curves, what unit economics means — LTV/CAC ratio thresholds that signal scalability vs. death

4.3M negotiation/sales community. Never Split the Difference (Chris Voss) sold 3M+ copies — proven massive appetite for negotiation science. No app gamifies daily negotiation science learning. Gap: one negotiation science concept per day (what tactical empathy is and why mirroring 'is that right?' produces more disclosure than questions, how the FBI discovered that 'no' is safer to pursue than 'yes' in high-stakes negotiation, what BATNA means and how to calculate your walkaway point in any deal

24M+ personal finance / business finance community. Khan Academy covers the basics but passive. No app teaches the daily science of business finance for founders and aspiring entrepreneurs. Gap: one business finance concept per day (what gross margin vs. contribution margin is and why SaaS companies optimize both differently, how to read a cash flow statement and why profitable companies go bankrupt — the cash conversion cycle, what burn rate and runway mean and how to calculate months to zero,

2.73M marketing community. 'Obviously Awesome' (April Dunford) and 'Traction' (Gabriel Weinberg) are perennial bestsellers — proven hunger for the science of marketing. No app gamifies daily marketing science learning. Gap: one marketing science concept per day (what Jobs-to-be-Done theory is and why Milkshake research revealed why customers really hire products, how positioning differs from messaging and why getting positioning wrong makes all downstream marketing fail, what the difference betw

6.6M leadership/business community. 'Leaders Eat Last' (Simon Sinek), 'The Five Dysfunctions of a Team' (Lencioni), 'Radical Candor' (Kim Scott) are massive sellers — proven appetite for leadership science. No app teaches daily leadership science as a streak habit. Gap: one leadership science concept per day (what psychological safety is per Amy Edmondson's research and how Google's Project Aristotle confirmed it as the #1 predictor of team performance, how situational leadership theory works —

6.5M productivity community. 'Deep Work' (Cal Newport) sold 1M+ copies, 'Atomic Habits' (James Clear) sold 15M+ copies — the single largest proven appetite for productivity science in any category. All existing apps are TOOLS (Todoist, Notion, Forest). None teach the SCIENCE of why productivity methods work. Gap: one productivity science concept per day (what attention residue is — Roy Baumeister and Gloria Mark proved task-switching leaves a cognitive residue lasting 20+ minutes reducing perfor

4.2M sales community. 'The Challenger Sale', 'SPIN Selling', 'To Sell Is Human' are perennial bestsellers — massive appetite for the science of sales. No app teaches daily sales science as a gamified habit. Gap: one sales science concept per day (what the Challenger Sale research found — 57% of top performers challenge customer thinking vs. 17% of relationship builders, how SPIN Selling works — the four question types (Situation/Problem/Implication/Need-Payoff) and why Implication questions 10x

6.3M strategy/business community. 'Good Strategy Bad Strategy' (Rumelt), 'Playing to Win' (Lafley/Martin), 'The Innovator's Dilemma' (Christensen) — proven massive appetite for strategic thinking frameworks. No app teaches daily business strategy science as a gamified habit. Gap: one strategy science concept per day (what Richard Rumelt's strategy kernel is and why 90% of 'strategies' are actually goals with no diagnosis or policy, how Porter's Five Forces identifies industry attractiveness vs.

6.4M psychology/economics community. 'Thinking Fast and Slow' (Kahneman) sold 10M+ copies — the single most validated appetite for behavioral economics science. No app gamifies daily behavioral economics learning as a streak habit. Gap: one behavioral economics concept per day (what Kahneman's System 1 vs. System 2 means and why intuition fails in statistically dense environments, how prospect theory explains why a $10 loss hurts 2.5x more than a $10 gain feels good — and what this means for pri

3.17M product/innovation community. 'Inspired' (Marty Cagan), 'The Mom Test' (Rob Fitzpatrick), 'Sprint' (Jake Knapp, Google Ventures) are perennial bestsellers — proven appetite for innovation science. No app teaches daily product innovation science as a streak habit. Gap: one innovation science concept per day (what the Innovator's Dilemma S-curve means and why incumbents rationally miss disruptions by focusing on profitable customers, how Jobs-to-be-Done interviews work — the 5 questions that
